mnemonics.title: SJ-DPS
mnemonics.content: StringJoiner Constructor Parameters: Delimiter, Prefix, Suffix. Remember 'SJ Needs DPS Support'

mnemonics.title: JARS Method
mnemonics.content: Joining Approaches Ranked: 1) String.join() 2) StringJoiner 3) += (Avoid). Remember 'JARS get heavier from right to left'
BAD Loop: 'Beware Appending in Loops'. Use StringJoiner instead of += in iterations
mnemonics.title: BAD Loop
DPS Over SOS
mnemonics.content: When needing Delimiter+Prefix+Suffix, choose StringJoiner (DPS) over manual String Operations (SOS)

acronyms.expansion: Just Use StringJoiner When Joining Objects Iteratively, Now
acronyms.usage: Remember to prefer StringJoiner for loop-based concatenation
StringJoiner workflow: Create instance, Set elements, Iterate results

visualTips.visualization: Picture writing on stone tablets - you must create a new tablet for every change
Imagine a train: Engine (prefix) + Carriages (strings) linked with couplings (delimiter) + Caboose (suffix)
Visualize StringJoiner as a shopping basket collecting items, versus += as making photocopies of your entire grocery list each time you add an item
See it as a zipper - combines elements (teeth) with a delimiter (zipper slider) in one motion

analogies.analogy: Like using a shopping cart (StringJoiner) vs carrying each item individually while shopping (+= operator)
Similar to punctuation in sentences - commas separate list items, just like delimiters separate strings
Like bookends on a shelf - they contain and define the collection of strings between them
Acts like a universal adapter plug - converts any data type to fit into the string joining system
